cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: LangChain and NVIDIA Launch NemoClaw Deep Agents Blueprint for Enterprise Agents
stories.plain: LangChain and NVIDIA released a reference architecture for building open enterprise agent systems, claiming 10x lower inference costs and benchmark-leading performance. This matters because a credible, low-cost blueprint from two major infrastructure players lowers the bar for enterprises to move agentic AI from experiment to production — directly shaping how marketing AI is built.

stories.head: The CMS is Becoming the AI Operating System for Brands
stories.plain: A MarTech analysis argues that choosing a CMS now means choosing your AI infrastructure — covering discovery, governance, personalization, and agents in one platform decision. This reframes a familiar purchase as a foundational AI-stack choice, with major implications for how marketing technology is bought and governed going forward.

stories.head: Angelfish Marketing Study of 1,052,053 AI Citations Maps What AI Reads to Recommend B2B Software
stories.plain: Analysing over a million AI citations, this study maps the exact sources AI assistants draw on when recommending B2B SaaS. For marketers, this is actionable intelligence on where to publish and earn authority so their brand surfaces inside AI-generated answers — directly shaping GEO strategy and content investment decisions.

stories.head: Akeneo Introduces the First Truly Agentic Product Experience Platform
stories.plain: Akeneo's Summer Release converts its Product Cloud from a passive data store into a fleet of coordinating agents that act autonomously on product content tasks. This signals a broader shift in MarTech: category-defining platforms are re-architecting around agentic AI, changing what buyers should evaluate and demand from vendors.

stories.head: Typeface Expands Ad and Video Agents, Previews Closed-Loop Optimization
stories.plain: Typeface upgraded its Ad and Video agents and previewed closed-loop optimization — meaning the system will soon automatically measure, learn, and adjust creative without human intervention. The closed-loop preview is significant: it moves generative content tools toward autonomous decisioning, blurring the line between creative AI and performance optimization.

stories.head: Angelfish Marketing study of 1M+ AI citations maps what AI reads to recommend B2B software
stories.plain: Analysing over a million AI citations, this research shows exactly which sources ChatGPT and peers draw on when recommending B2B SaaS tools. For marketers, it is the first large-scale evidence base for generative engine optimisation — revealing where to publish and earn links if you want your brand surfaced inside AI answers, not just Google results.

stories.head: The CMS is becoming the AI operating system for brands
stories.plain: A strategic piece arguing that CMS buying decisions now lock in your AI readiness — covering discovery, personalisation, governance, and agentic workflows. It reframes the CMS as infrastructure for AI, not just publishing, which changes how marketing technology is evaluated and purchased across the stack.

stories.head: LangChain and NVIDIA launch NemoClaw Deep Agents blueprint for enterprise AI agents
stories.plain: LangChain and NVIDIA released a reference architecture for building open enterprise agent systems, claiming 10x lower inference costs than alternatives. This matters because it lowers the financial barrier to deploying agentic AI in production marketing and business workflows, shifting the build-vs-buy calculus for enterprise teams.

stories.head: Typeface expands Ad and Video Agents, previews closed-loop optimisation
stories.plain: Typeface's June release upgrades its Ad and Video agents and previews closed-loop optimisation — where the system measures creative performance and feeds results back to improve future outputs automatically. This is a practical step toward agentic decisioning in paid media, moving beyond generate-and-send to a self-improving creative loop.

stories.head: How AI discovery is changing everything marketers measure
stories.plain: As AI assistants replace search clicks, traffic-based metrics like sessions and CTR are losing meaning. This piece explains what signals to track instead — including brand mentions inside AI answers and share of model-generated recommendations — reframing how marketing performance is reported to leadership.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Search Is the New Visibility Battleground
body: A study of over one million AI citations reveals that ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity pull recommendations from a surprisingly narrow set of trusted third-party sources — analyst reports, review sites, and authoritative editorial. For B2B brands, this means traditional SEO tactics are no longer sufficient; you must engineer presence in the specific content formats AI models actually ingest.

The implication is a new discipline — sometimes called Generative Engine Optimization — where trust signals replace keyword density as the primary visibility lever. Brands that do not audit where they appear in AI training and retrieval pipelines risk disappearing from the consideration set entirely, even if their web traffic looks healthy.

themes.head: Agentic Platforms Move from Prototype to Production
body: LangChain and NVIDIA's NemoClaw blueprint, Akeneo's agentic product-experience platform, and Typeface's closed-loop ad & video agents all shipped within the same week — signalling that multi-step autonomous agents are moving into production workflows rather than staying in pilot. These are not chatbots; they execute multi-tool tasks across live data without human hand-holding at each step.

Wistia's MCP server — letting Claude and ChatGPT reach directly into a brand's video library — illustrates a wider pattern: marketing platforms are exposing APIs designed specifically for AI agents to call. The architectural question is no longer 'should we build agents?' but 'which platforms in our stack are agent-ready today?'

themes.head: The CMS Becomes the AI Operating System
body: Content management systems are being re-architected as the central orchestration layer for brand AI — not just storing copy but routing tasks to agents, managing context, and enforcing brand governance at scale. This reframes the CMS purchase from a publishing tool to infrastructure-level infrastructure, on par with a CDP or a data warehouse.

HubSpot's Breeze updates and the Hermes Agent Desktop pattern both point in the same direction: workflow automation and content operations are converging into a single AI-native surface. Buyers evaluating any core platform now need to ask whether it can serve as an orchestration hub, not just a record of truth.

themes.head: Stack Consolidation Pressure Hits a Tipping Point
body: Multiple pieces this month converge on a single uncomfortable truth: fragmented stacks have a measurable, hidden cost — in data latency, duplicated vendor fees, and the integration overhead that blocks AI from working across tools. The 'more vendors' era is being replaced by a 'make the stack work harder' mandate from CFOs and CMOs alike.

The practical guidance is to audit integration depth, not feature breadth — a smaller set of deeply connected platforms outperforms a sprawling set of best-of-breed point solutions once AI agents need to move data across them. Rokt-mParticle's framing of performance marketing as a stack-efficiency problem is representative of how the conversation has shifted.

themes.head: AI That Guides, Not Controls, Wins Consumer Trust
body: Research on AI-driven financial decisions shows consumers actively resist fully autonomous AI — they want recommendations and guardrails, not a system that acts without them. The same dynamic appears in retail, where Furniture.com's leadership describes AI's job as helping customers navigate overwhelming choice rather than making choices for them.

For marketers, this is a design constraint, not just a trust & safety checkbox: AI-powered experiences that over-automate or obscure the decision process generate abandonment, not conversion. The winning pattern is 'guided autonomy' — AI narrows the option set and explains its reasoning, while the human retains the final action.
ev: Agile Brand · Furniture.com on endless choice

month.takeaway: Every major shift this month — from AI search visibility to agentic platforms to stack consolidation — points to the same inflection: the brands that will win are those that re-architect around AI as infrastructure, not as a feature layered on top of legacy processes.
